beats.narration: Radiography hangs on one question: what happens when a photon meets matter? Through, absorbed, or scattered. Three mechanisms decide everything.

payload.title: PHOTON VS MATTER
payload.sub: Three interactions decide every radiograph you'll ever read.

beats.narration: Knock an electron clean out: free electron, positive ion — an ion pair. Ionization forms the image, drives survey meters, harms living tissue.
Photoelectric: the photon vanishes, totally absorbed. Compton: it survives, scattered with less energy. Above 1.022 mega electron volts, pair production makes an electron-positron pair.
Low energy, high atomic number: photoelectric rules. That's subject contrast. Compton owns the working middle: scatter and fog. Pair production matters at megavoltage.
So memorize the rule: photon energy and atomic number pick the winner. Learn which effect owns which territory, and radiographic technique starts making sense.

payload.sub: Together they pick the winning interaction.
